[2020-10-02 21:16] terrareveene (Terra Reveene): Fusion Drone
1:Gold::Energy::Drone::Engineer: — 3:Time:, 10🌱, 2:Health:, :Blocker: 
Start of turn: Gain 3:Gold::Energy:

Accelerator Drone
1:Gold::Energy:— 1 :Health:, 4🌱
Start of turn: Construct 1 barrier for your opponent, gain 1:Gold:

I put some more thought into these this time.
I narrowed down Fusion Drone to be strictly about being a combination of an engineer and a drone and nothing else (perhaps being used as your first absorber once or twice for the early game, or in certain red sets where you might be able to get away with this as your primary absorber? Also there to power up certain units that want to consume a lot of energy consistently) (1DE - 1DE - 1DE - 1DDE - 1DDDE, as an example opener for P1)
I scaled down Accelerator Drone to a point where it's actively bad late game, but allows you to build a huge economy very quickly (11EE - 11DD - DDDD, as an example opener for P1).
Would love to hear some thoughts! Good/bad? Fun/un-fun? Interesting/uninteresting? P1/P2 favoring or nah? Good idea/mediocre idea?

[2020-10-02 21:48] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Fusion Drone is 36% inflation/rate of return/whatever we used to call it, if you account for the lost energy and ignore the defense swing.  Colorless absorbers kinda break an unwritten rule, but I guess the long build time makes it less of an issue?  10 supply seems excessive for a double drone, and since the first one is amazing and after that they are a bit awkward (and you probably aren't gambitting  them like you might with Boreholes), I'd lower it to 4.
Accelerator Drone has a few issues: 1) having the effect as "start of turn" makes it break SDR, since there will be situations where being given a free Accelerator Drone could cause you to lose, 2) even if you convert it to a click effect, it makes defense math awkward for the other player, 3) it would probably be busted as a click effect so idk how to save it

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: I guess you *could* make it a click ability

messages.author: randomflyingtaco
messages.content: Double drone (giving 2 gold more than the components) is fine, but units like that are typically limited to 4 supply (Thorium, old Vivid, old Wild).

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: It feels a little weird though to me, it kind of undermines the point of the drone

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: it becomes a dead card once the barrier isnt worth it

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: I was referring to the 10 supply double drone, though I forget its name

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: or if you really want to drive it home

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: click: gain 2 gold and give your opponent a husk

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: I wanted the drone to artificially increase your opponent's absorb value by 1 in exchange for a highly discounted drone

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: If it's changed to a click ability, it no longer does that, but instead does something else

messages.author: randomflyingtaco
messages.content: 36% inflation means that it is (one of?) the best econ unit(s).  Normally econ units are down in the 30-35% range and only exceed that if they are stronger in a specific situation.

messages.author: randomflyingtaco
messages.content: I agree that changing it to a click ability guts the idea of the unit, but breaking SDR is a non-starter for the majority of the community, so you won't get much discussion on the "start of turn" version other than people telling you to make it into a different unit.

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: I see, so it's slightly better than upper range for normal drones then? I could see that

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: And yeah, I'm seeing that, about the SDR

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: Is there anything specific about breaking SDR in this case that would be bothersome? Or is it just the breaking of SDR in general that is off-putting?

messages.author: randomflyingtaco
messages.content: On top of being a vigilant, colorless absorber.  So, you need to pick which part of the identity is more important: efficient long-build-time drone or vigilant colorless absorber. imo

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: drone is 28% or 33% depending on if you count energy

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: Hmm, alright, I think having a colorless vigilant absorber is quite a cool identity, so that'd probably be the way that I'd go

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: If I were to want to decrease its efficiency, say to be a build time 2 drone, what kind of cost would you think is proper for it?

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: Or would it be better off as some sort of build time 1 drone?

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: colorless vigialant absorber :NotLikeDuck:

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: Counting inflation isn't my biggest strength 😅

messages.author: randomflyingtaco
messages.content:
unfortunately prismata has a pretty narrow/tightly balanced design space and you can't just sideboard something to fix balance issues, so numbers are difficult to get exactly right.
Like I mentioned in my previous post, the high build time was what made colorless absorber more tolerable, so I'd advise against lowering it to 1

messages.author: jamberine
messages.content: what's wrong with colorless absorber

messages.author: randomflyingtaco
messages.content: Mega costs attack and there are no colorless attackers? (not counting savior btw)

messages.author: randomflyingtaco
messages.content: The point being that a colorless absorber lets you delay committing to a tech, but you should probably get a better player's opinion on the matter

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: Why would a build time 1 colorless vigilant defender be unbearable? I don't see it being mentioned in the previous post ("... So you need to pick which part of the identity...")

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: Or rather, looking + reading while typing :d

messages.author: randomflyingtaco
messages.content: Anyway, here is the spreadsheet I used to use to make sure my unit numbers are reasonable https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1BJhRnU6JV_lHzir898Gbu8jE4f-CASdgd3YmTa8xdUs/edit?usp=sharing

messages.author: terrareveene
messages.content: oooh, that looks really handy, thank you!

messages.author: randomflyingtaco
messages.content: It's not perfect, but it's a reasonable reference for "should my attacker cost 7B or 9B?" beyond just having an pr&i or trying to construct it from other units. absorbers are a bit more difficult to model

[2020-10-22 21:48] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): I woke up thinking about a unit with :Chill: 99 on target unit that already has :Chill: on it. Went through a couple of variations like :Chill: 99 on target unit with :Lifespan: 1 but eventually settled on:
Veritania, Queen of Obnoxious Chill Indicators (10 :Blue: :Red: :Red: , 1 Supply) 10 :Health: 
:Frontline: 
:Click: : :Chill: 10 on target unit that already has :Chill: , otherwise consume an Animus to create 3 Frostbites with :Lifespan: 1.
[2020-10-22 21:53] iminabearsuit: It seems kind of bad.
[2020-10-22 21:53] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): a) yes, I know that you would Frostbite something, Veritania it, and then unclick the Frostbite to chill another target, but I thought adding a bunch of chill to a partially chilled unit was more interesting than adding a multi-turn chill status (and having it just be exhaust would mean that you could exhaust attackers with blocker, breaking SDR)
b) yes, I know that no other units currently have context-dependent click effects but I didn't want it to be useless if you didn't roll any chill in the set
c) yes, it costs BRR on purpose because I have the sense of humor of a 12 year-old
[2020-10-22 21:54] .bky_1556 (bky): :Click: : Create something with :Lifespan: 1 means it's impossible to click the created unit before it expires.
[2020-10-22 21:55] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Oh, I didn't bother figuring out cost and just went with 10 gold, 10 hp, 10 chill.  Figure actual numbers after I decide whether there is any scrap of the idea worth saving
[2020-10-22 21:55] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): @bky Oh, is that how it goes? I always forget when exhaust+lifespan tick down.
[2020-10-22 21:57] .bky_1556 (bky): actually, given frost brooder behavior, I'm not sure.
[2020-10-22 21:58] iminabearsuit: Build time one, delays the life span
[2020-10-22 21:58] .bky_1556 (bky): yeah, you show the lifespan and the delay until it starts ticking separately
[2020-10-22 22:00] iminabearsuit: Maybe it's not that bad, it's a lot of pressure for cheap out of nowhere. But it's just very awkward.
[2020-10-22 22:03] iminabearsuit: I think a cleaner version would probably be.
:Click: Double/tripple the amount of freeze on a unit.
When bought, construct a Frostbite with :Exhaust:1 and a Frostbite with :Exhaust: 2.
[2020-10-22 22:03] .bky_1556 (bky): What does the error message say when you hit an unchilled target and don't have an Animus?
[2020-10-22 22:03] iminabearsuit: Instead of having all these weird wordings.
[2020-10-22 22:07] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): any version of it breaks ordering rules ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
